subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – In the Kingdom of Fools
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Once there was a kingdom ruled by a foolish king and his equally foolish minister. They made strange laws that no other kingdom had ever followed. The king ordered that everyone must sleep during the day and remain awake at night. Anyone who disobeyed this order would be punished severely. Out of fear, the people of the kingdom followed the rule.
questions: Who ruled the kingdom?
What kind of laws did the king make?
What order did the king give about day and night?
What punishment was given for disobedience?
Why did the people follow the rule?

passages.paragraph: One day, a guru and his disciple arrived at the gates of the city. They found the streets empty because everyone was asleep during the day. As night approached, the city slowly came alive. Shops opened, people began working, and markets became busy. The guru found this system strange and suspicious.
questions: Who arrived at the city?
Why were the streets empty during the day?
What happened as night approached?
What activities began at night?
How did the guru feel about the system?

passages.paragraph: When the disciple visited the market, he noticed something very unusual. Everything in the market cost the same price, one duddu. Rice, bananas, vegetables, and even expensive items were sold at the same price. The disciple was delighted by this discovery.
questions: Where did the disciple go?
What unusual thing did he notice?
How much did all goods cost?
Name two items sold in the market.
How did the disciple react?

passages.paragraph: The guru immediately realised that the kingdom was ruled by fools. He warned his disciple that living in such a kingdom was dangerous. He advised him to leave the place immediately. However, the disciple refused because he enjoyed the cheap food available in the market.
questions: What did the guru realise about the kingdom?
What warning did he give the disciple?
What advice did he give?
Why did the disciple refuse to leave?
What attracted the disciple to stay?

passages.paragraph: After some time, a thief broke into a merchant’s house through a weak wall. While escaping with stolen goods, the wall collapsed and fell on him, killing him instantly. The thief’s brother complained to the king and demanded justice.
questions: Who broke into the merchant’s house?
How did the thief enter the house?
What happened while he was escaping?
Who complained to the king?
What did he demand?

passages.paragraph: The king ordered an investigation into the matter. The merchant blamed the bricklayer who had built the wall. The bricklayer blamed a dancing girl who distracted him with the sound of her anklets. The dancer blamed the goldsmith for delaying her jewellery.
questions: What did the king order?
Who did the merchant blame?
Who did the bricklayer blame?
Why did the bricklayer blame the dancer?
Who did the dancer blame?

passages.paragraph: Finally, the goldsmith explained that he had delayed the dancer’s jewellery because he was busy completing an order for the merchant. The king concluded that the merchant was responsible for the thief’s death and ordered his execution.
questions: What explanation did the goldsmith give?
Why was the jewellery delayed?
Who was blamed again?
What decision did the king make?
What punishment was ordered?

passages.paragraph: However, when the time came for execution, the merchant was too thin to fit the execution stake. The king’s servants searched the town for someone fat enough. Soon they found the disciple, who had become very fat from eating cheap food.
questions: Why could the merchant not be executed?
What did the king’s servants do next?
What kind of person were they searching for?
Whom did they find?
Why had the disciple become fat?

passages.paragraph: At that moment, the guru arrived to save his disciple. He told the king that whoever died first on the stake would be reborn as king in the next life. Hearing this, the greedy king decided that he and his minister should die first.
questions: Who arrived to save the disciple?
What did the guru tell the king?
What would happen to the first person who died?
How did the king react?
Who decided to die first?

passages.paragraph: The king and minister secretly replaced the guru and disciple and were executed by the guards. The people were shocked to learn that their rulers were dead. They requested the guru to become their king. The guru agreed on the condition that he would change all the foolish laws and restore normal life in the kingdom.
questions: Who replaced the guru and disciple?
What happened to the king and minister?
How did the people react?
Whom did the people choose as the new king?
What condition did the guru place before ruling?
